title: `route:cache` mutates live route instances, breaking subsequent `optimize` tasks that analyze routes
author: hosni
body: ### Laravel Version 13.19.0 ### PHP Version 8.4.23 ### Database Driver & Version N/A (not database-related) ### Description `RouteCacheCommand` calls `prepareForSerialization()` directly on route instances returned from `getFreshApplicationRoutes()`. https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/3093ff3a61f88225f16fec35dda65e1e7c0867a4/src/Illuminate/Foundation/Console/RouteCacheCommand.php#L56-L64 `Route::prepareForSerialization()` intentionally removes runtime dependencies: https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/3093ff3a61f88225f16fec35dda65e1e7c0867a4/src/Illuminate/Routing/Route.php#L1560 Those route objects remain referenced by the live router collection. Later in the same PHP process, any code that still needs to analyze routes can fail with: ``` LogicException: Route is not bound. ``` This happens because after `container` is unset. Access like `$route->container` goes through `__get()`: https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/3093ff3a61f88225f16fec35dda65e1e7c0867a4/src/Illuminate/Routing/Route.php#L1569-L1573 which calls `parameters()`: https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/3093ff3a61f88225f16fec35dda65e1e7c0867a4/src/Illuminate/Routing/Route.php#L432-L435 And that throws when the route was never bound to a request: https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/3093ff3a61f88225f16fec35dda65e1e7c0867a4/src/Illuminate/Routing/Route.php#L483-L490 This is especially visible with `php artisan optimize`, which runs built-in cache commands before `ServiceProvider::$optimizeCommands`: ```php [ 'config' => 'config:cache', 'events' => 'event:cache', 'routes' => 'route:cache', 'views' => 'view:cache', ...ServiceProvider::$optimizeCommands, ] ``` If a package registers an optimize task that analyzes routes (for example, to cache generated metadata), that task can fail after `route:cache` has already run in the same `optimize` command. ### Steps To Reproduce **Reproduction repository:** https://github.com/hosni/laravel-optimize-command-bug 1. Clone the reproduction repository: ```bash git clone https://github.com/hosni/laravel-optimize-command-bug.git cd laravel-optimize-command-bug composer install cp .env.example .env php artisan key:generate ``` 2. The app registers a custom optimize command via `AppServiceProvider`: ```php $this->optimizes( optimize: 'test:route-analysis', key: 'route-analysis', ); ``` 3. The `test:route-analysis` command iterates all routes and analyzes them: ```php foreach (Route::getRoutes() as $route) { dump($route->uri(), $route->getController(), $route->gatherMiddleware()); } ``` 4. Run the command directly ŌĆö it succeeds: ```bash php artisan test:route-analysis ``` 5. Run it as part of `optimize` ŌĆö it fails: ```bash php artisan optimize ``` **Expected:** `optimize` completes successfully, including the `route-analysis` task. **Actual:** The `route-analysis` task fails with: ``` LogicException: Route is not bound. ``` The failure occurs on any route when `gatherMiddleware()` is called after `route:cache` has already executed in the same process. **Workaround:** ```bash php artisan optimize --except=route-analysis php artisan test:route-analysis ``` **Proposed fix:** Clone each route before calling `prepareForSerialization()`, and build the route cache file from the cloned/prepared collection so live route instances are not mutated.

title: Dev command terminates on Windows because Pail require pcntl extension
author: avvertix
body:
### Laravel Version

13.19.0

### PHP Version

8.5.7 (NTS Visual C++ 2022 x64)

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

I'm trying the `php artisan dev` on Windows 11 (the actual version doesn't matter). 

The `dev` command execution stop immediately after launch because Laravel Pail command requires the `pcntl` extension to run, which is not installable on Windows.

```bash
php artisan dev       
```

Below the traces from the stdout. I separated them in blocks to hopefully make them more readable

```bash
[server] php artisan serve --host=localhost
[queue]  php artisan queue:listen --tries=1 --timeout=0
[logs]   php artisan pail --timeout=0
[vite]   npm run dev
```

```bash
[queue] 
    INFO  Processing jobs from the [default] queue.  
```

```bash
[vite] 
 > dev
 > vite
```

```bash
[logs] 
    RuntimeException 
 
   The [pcntl] extension is required to run Pail.
 
   at vendor\laravel\pail\src\Guards\EnsurePcntlIsAvailable.php:15
      11Ō¢Ģ      */
      12Ō¢Ģ     public static function check(): void
      13Ō¢Ģ     {
      14Ō¢Ģ         if (! function_exists('pcntl_fork')) {
   Ō×£  15Ō¢Ģ             throw new RuntimeException('The [pcntl] extension is required to run Pail.');
      16Ō¢Ģ         }
      17Ō¢Ģ     }
      18Ō¢Ģ }
      19Ō¢Ģ
 
   1   vendor\laravel\pail\src\Console\Commands\PailCommand.php:46
       Laravel\Pail\Guards\EnsurePcntlIsAvailable::check()
 
   2   vendor\laravel\framework\src\Illuminate\Container\BoundMethod.php:36
       Laravel\Pail\Console\Commands\PailCommand::handle(Object(Laravel\Pail\ProcessFactory))
 
 php artisan pail --timeout=0 exited with code 1
--> Sending SIGTERM to other processes..
```

```bash
[server] 
    INFO  Server running on [http://localhost:8000].  
 
   Press Ctrl+C to stop the server
``` 

```bash
[queue] php artisan queue:listen --tries=1 --timeout=0 exited with code 1
--> Sending SIGTERM to other processes..
[server] php artisan serve --host=localhost exited with code 1
--> Sending SIGTERM to other processes..
[vite] npm run dev exited with code 1
```

### Steps To Reproduce

- In a existing Laravel application upgrade laravel/framework to at least v13.17.0 (v13.19.0 in my case). A fresh installation does not need preparatory steps
- Run `php artisan dev`
- Observe that `pail` command terminates with exception and other commands are killed as consequence
- Commenting or removing line [`self::artisan('pail --timeout=0', 'logs');`]( https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/3093ff3a61f88225f16fec35dda65e1e7c0867a4/src/Illuminate/Foundation/DevCommands.php#L60) in DevCommands class let the processes stay up

title: TrustProxies with at: * does not work correctly when request has passed through multiple proxies
author: mattford
body:
### Laravel Version

12.63.0, 13.x

### PHP Version

8.4

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

I have an application running on ELB behind a CloudFront distribution. Both ELB and CloudFront forward the request downstream resulting in my X-Forwarded-For header looking like this:

`1.1.1.1, 2.2.2.2`

Where `1.1.1.1` is my actual client IP address, 2.2.2.2 is CloudFront, and 3.3.3.3 is ELB.

I have trusted all proxies with the line:
```
$middleware->trustProxies(
            at: '*',
            headers: Request::HEADER_X_FORWARDED_FOR |
            Request::HEADER_X_FORWARDED_HOST |
            Request::HEADER_X_FORWARDED_PORT |
            Request::HEADER_X_FORWARDED_PROTO |
            Request::HEADER_X_FORWARDED_AWS_ELB
        );
```

However when I call `$request->ip()` I get `2.2.2.2`

I've dug into this a bit and discovered this is because when Laravel trusts all proxies, it sets the list of trusted IPs to the REMOTE_ADDR (3.3.3.3 in my case). 
This list ends up getting passed to Symfony, which then returns the latest untrusted IP in the list, which is `2.2.2.2`.

As Symfony does not support wildcards, my proposed fix for this is to gather a list of all X-FORWARDED-FOR and FORWARDED IP addresses, and set them into the $trustedProxies array, so that Symfony trusts the entire chain and will therefore return the first trusted IP, 1.1.1.1 in my case.

I note that there is a test case for this but it appears to be incorrect, according to https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Headers/X-Forwarded-For the leftmost IP is the original client IP, which reflects in what I am seeing.

I have a fix in progress for this, will raise a PR

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Start a Laravel app with TrustProxies:* 
2. Craft a request with multiple IP addresses in X-FORWARDED-FOR or FORWARDED header
3. Observe that the last IP in the list is used as the original client IP, not the first!

title: Incorrect return type for `Illuminate\Console\Concerns\HasParameters::getOptions()`
author: wimski
body:
### Laravel Version

13.19.0

### PHP Version

8.5.8

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

`Illuminate\Console\Concerns\HasParameters::getOptions()` has the following return type as defined by a docblock annotation:

```php
    /**
     * Get the console command options.
     *
     * @return (InputOption|array{
     *    0: non-empty-string,
     *    1?: string|non-empty-array<string>,
     *    2?: int-mask-of<InputOption::VALUE_*>,
     *    3?: string,
     *    4?: mixed,
     *    5?: list<string|Suggestion>|\Closure(CompletionInput, CompletionSuggestions): list<string|Suggestion>
     * })[]
     */
```
https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/13.x/src/Illuminate/Console/Concerns/HasParameters.php#L56-L67

If the output of this method is an array, then the output will be used to feed the method `Symfony\Component\Console\Command\Command::addOption()` which has the following signature:

```php
public function addOption(
    string $name,
    string|array|null $shortcut = null,
    ?int $mode = null,
    string $description = '',
    mixed $default = null,
    array|\Closure $suggestedValues = [],
): static
```
https://github.com/symfony/symfony/blob/8.2/src/Symfony/Component/Console/Command/Command.php#L434

Symfony accepts `null` for the shortcut argument, but Laravel says it has to be `string|non-empty-array<string>` without the `null` option. So a `null` value is valid and will work at runtime, but static analysis will complain (rightfully so).

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Create a console command class.
2. Add the `getOptions` method returning an entry with the shortcut value set to `null`.
3. Run static analysis.

### Example

#### Command
```php
class MyCommand extends Command
{
    protected function getOptions(): array
    {
        return [
            ['name', null, InputOption::VALUE_OPTIONAL, 'description'],
        ];
    }
}
```

#### Output
```
 ------ ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
  Line   app/Console/Commands/MyCommand.php                                                                                                                                  
 ------ ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
    5    Method App\Console\Commands\MyCommand::getOptions() should return array<array{0: non-empty-string, 1?: non-empty-array<string>|string, 2?: int<0, 31>, 3?: string, 4?: mixed, 5?:
         (Closure(Symfony\Component\Console\Completion\CompletionInput, Symfony\Component\Console\Completion\CompletionSuggestions): list<string|Symfony\Component\Console\Completion
         \Suggestion>)|list<string|Symfony\Component\Console\Completion\Suggestion>}|Symfony\Component\Console\Input\InputOption> but returns array{array{'name', null, 4, 'description'}}.                                                                                               
         ­¤¬¬  return.type                                                                                                                                                                      
         ­¤ÆĪ  Type #1 from the union: Offset 1 (non-empty-array<int|string, string>|string) does not accept type null: Type #1 from the union: null is empty.                                  
 ------ -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

title: Default `serializable_classes = false` prevents caching Eloquent models without additional configuration
author: akram-khodami
body:
### Laravel Version

13.12.0

### PHP Version

8.3.31

### Database Driver & Version

MySQL

### Cache Driver

database

### Description

With a fresh Laravel 13 installation, the default cache configuration contains:

```php
'serializable_classes' => false,
```

When caching Eloquent models or Eloquent collections using the Database cache driver, the cached value cannot be restored correctly with the default configuration.

For example:

```php
Cache::remember('products', 3600, function () {
    return Product::latest()->get();
});

$products = Cache::get('products');

dd($products);
```

Instead of returning an `Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Collection` containing `Product` models, the cached value contains `__PHP_Incomplete_Class` instances.

As a result, attempting to access model properties results in errors such as:

```
Attempt to read property "name" on string
```

or inspecting the cached value shows `__PHP_Incomplete_Class` objects.

The issue disappears after explicitly allowing the serialized classes, for example:

```php
'serializable_classes' => [
    Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Collection::class,
    App\Models\Product::class,
]
```

After clearing the cache and configuration, the same code works correctly.

### Expected Behavior

I expected one of the following:

- Caching Eloquent models to work out of the box with the default configuration, or
- The default configuration or documentation to clearly indicate that Eloquent model classes must be explicitly listed in `serializable_classes`.

### Question

Is this the intended behavior?

If so, perhaps the default configuration or documentation could better explain that Eloquent models cannot be cached unless they are explicitly allowed.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Create a fresh Laravel 13.12.0 application.
2. Configure the cache driver:

```env
CACHE_STORE=database
```

3. Create a basic `Product` model and migration.
4. Insert at least one product into the database.
5. Cache an Eloquent collection:

```php
Cache::remember('products', 3600, function () {
    return Product::latest()->get();
});
```

6. Retrieve the cached value:

```php
$products = Cache::get('products');

dd($products);
```

7. Observe that the cached value contains `__PHP_Incomplete_Class` objects when the default configuration is:

```php
'serializable_classes' => false,
```

8. Update the configuration:

```php
'serializable_classes' => [
    Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Collection::class,
    App\Models\Product::class,
]
```

9. Clear the cache and configuration:

```bash
php artisan optimize:clear
```

10. Repeat steps 5 and 6.

The cached collection is now restored correctly as an `Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Collection` containing `Product` model instances.

title: Resource does not resolve correctly if Model's directory contains the model name
author: Rubrasum
body:
### Laravel Version

13.7.0

### PHP Version

8.4

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

If your model exists in a directory with a name that includes the model name (EX Jumping/Jump) the resource resolves to the model's path but excludes the directory.

I recreated this with different models/directories and if the model is included in the directory name, it fails each time. 

### Steps To Reproduce


Sample Model in `App/Models/BAM/Jumping/Jump`
Sample model in `App/Models/BAM/Jumping/Hop`

Then in tinker do guessResourceName() for each and it gives the wrong path for Jump:

```
> Jump::guessResourceName()

= [
    "App\\Http\\Resources\\BAM\\JumpResource",
    "App\\Http\\Resources\\BAM\\Jump",
  ]

> Hop::guessResourceName()

= [
    "App\\Http\\Resources\\BAM\\Jumping\\HopResource",
    "App\\Http\\Resources\\BAM\\Jumping\\Hop",
  ]
```

title: Route cache causes __PHP_Incomplete_Class error for controllers that reference $this via first-class callable syntax
author: chescos
body:
### Laravel Version

v13.19.0

### PHP Version

8.5.5

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

 When using first-class callable syntax for route definitions (e.g. `(new ContainerController)->show(...)`), running `php artisan route:cache` can produce an `__PHP_Incomplete_Class` error at runtime
   for controllers whose action method calls any method on `$this`.
   
  The chain of failure:
  
  1. `(new SomeController)->show(...)` creates a Closure with `$this` bound to the controller instance.
  2. `laravel/serializable-closure` v2's `ReflectionClosure::isBindingRequired()` analyzes the closure body. If the method calls `$this->anyMethod()`, it returns `true`, causing the bound controller
  instance to be serialized into the route cache as a raw PHP object (`O:N:"App\...\SomeController":0:{}`).
  3. When the route is dispatched, `Route::runCallable()` deserializes the cached closure using `unserialize()` with an `allowed_classes` whitelist that only permits internal serializable-closure
  classes (`SerializableClosure`, `Native`, `Signed`, `SelfReference`, `UnsignedSerializableClosure`).
  4. The controller class is not in the whitelist, so PHP produces `__PHP_Incomplete_Class` for `$this`.
  5. Calling any method on `$this` inside the route action throws a fatal `Error`.
  
  Critically, this only affects controllers whose action method calls `$this->method()` ŌĆö controllers that never reference `$this` in the action body get `"this":N;` (null) in the serialized form and
  are unaffected. This makes the bug appear to affect only specific routes, not all routes.
  
  The error produced:
```
  Error: The script tried to call a method on an incomplete object.
  Please ensure that the class definition "App\Http\Controllers...\SomeController"
  of the object you are trying to operate on was loaded before unserialize()
  gets called or provide an autoloader to load the class definition
```

  The issue lies at the intersection of two components:
  - `laravel/serializable-closure` serializes the bound `$this` object as a raw PHP serialized object, not as a `SerializableClosure`, so it is subject to the `allowed_classes` restriction.
  - `Route::runCallable()` in `laravel/framework` restricts `unserialize()` with `allowed_classes` that does not (and cannot generically) include user-defined controller classes.

  A workaround is to ensure the action method never uses the `$this` token ŌĆö e.g. by making any helper methods `static` and calling them via `self::`. This causes `isBindingRequired()` to return
  `false` and the controller is not serialized.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Create a controller with a route action that calls a private/public method on `$this`:
  
  ```php
  // app/Http/Controllers/FooController.php
  class FooController extends Controller
  {
      public function show(): string
      {
          return $this->greeting();
      }   
      
      private function greeting(): string
      {
          return 'hello';
      }
  }
  ```
  2. Register the route using first-class callable syntax:
```php
  // routes/web.php
  Route::get('/foo', (new FooController)->show(...));
```

  3. Run php artisan route:cache.
  4. Visit /foo.

  Expected: Page loads correctly, returns "hello".

  Actual: Fatal error:
  Error: The script tried to call a method on an incomplete object.
  Please ensure that the class definition "App\Http\Controllers\FooController"
  of the object you are trying to operate on was loaded _before_ unserialize() gets called.
  
  5. Run php artisan route:clear ŌĆö error disappears.
  6. Run php artisan route:cache again ŌĆö error returns.
  
  Root cause confirmation: inspect bootstrap/cache/routes-v7.php after step 3. You will see:
```
  "this";O:35:"App\Http\Controllers\FooController":0:{}
  The controller is embedded as a raw PHP serialized object. Change greeting() to static and call it as self::greeting() ŌĆö after re-caching, the entry becomes "this";N; and the error is gone.
  ```

title: `Storage::append()` and `Storage::prepend()` facade docblocks missing `$separator` parameter
author: JulianGlueck
body:
### Laravel Version

13.18.1

### PHP Version

8.5.8

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

I noticed that `FilesystemAdapter::append()` and `FilesystemAdapter::prepend()` both accept an optional third `$separator` parameter:

```php
public function append($path, $data, $separator = PHP_EOL) { ... }
public function prepend($path, $data, $separator = PHP_EOL) { ... }
```

However, the `@method` docblocks on the [Storage facade](https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/13.x/src/Illuminate/Support/Facades/Storage.php) only declare two parameters:

```php
@method static bool prepend(string $path, string $data)
@method static bool append(string $path, string $data)
```

This causes static analysis tools and IDEs to flag any usage of the third parameter as an error.

I wasn't sure if this is intentional (e.g. to discourage reliance on `$separator` through the facade) or simply an oversight in the docblock. If it's unintentional, would a PR updating the facade docblocks to include the parameter be welcome?

### Steps To Reproduce

Use `Storage::append` providing a third parameter and observe your IDE highlighting this as an error.

title: Size rules are broken when float are decoded to infinite values
author: bilboque
body:
### Laravel Version

13.x

### PHP Version

8.5

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

Very large json float values are decoded as INF or -INF. 
This result in an 500 instead of a validation failure for rules that will performs a comparaison e.g.: `min`, `max` ... .

Adding an additional check in `ensureExponentWithinAllowedRange` could prevent those 500 for those extreme values.

### Steps To Reproduce

For exemple use a form request with a size rule e.g: `['Size', 'Between', 'Min', 'Max', 'Gt', 'Lt', 'Gte', 'Lte']`
```php
    public function rules(): array
    {
        return [
            'value' => 'int|min:0',
        ];
    }
```

then query an endpoint that use the FormRequest with a json body like
```json
{
    "value": 1e500
}
```

title: Blade return complete view for missing fragments
author: royvanv
body:
### Laravel Version

13.18.1

### PHP Version

8.5.0

### Database Driver & Version

N/A

### Description

If you request any non-existent [fragment](https://laravel.com/docs/13.x/blade#rendering-blade-fragments) in Blade, the complete views is returned. I would expect an empty string or `null` to be returned instead, or even an exception.

Assuming there is no fragment with `x` defined, all these statements return the full view:
```php
view('welcome')->fragment('x'),
view('welcome')->fragments(['x']),
view('welcome')->fragmentIf(true, 'x'),
view('welcome')->fragmentsIf(true, ['x']),
```

This issue occurs because of the `null` check in [Illuminate\View\View on line 169](https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/27b256082fb4a5ceed6b658c65e3eaddf1f10222/src/Illuminate/View/View.php#L169).

### Steps To Reproduce

Create a view with the following content (e.g. `resources/views/welcome.blade.php`): 

```blade
@fragment('a')
    A
@endfragment

This should not show.

@fragment('a')
    B
@endfragment
```

Define a route (e.g. `/`) that returns fragments of the view:

```php
Route::get('/', function () {
     return view('welcome')
        ->fragments(['a', 'b', 'c']); // Fragment 'c' does not exist.
});
```

Open the route in a browser and see the output. It contains both fragment A and B and the text "This should not show." that's between them.

If you remove the non-existing fragment 'c' from the array, it works correctly.

title: No SSRF protection for HTTP client.
author: mantas-done
body:
### Laravel Version

13

### PHP Version

8.5

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

When a user-controlled $url is passed directly to Http::get(), it can be abused for SSRF attacks. An attacker can probe ports and internal services on your server (for example, checking whether MySQL is listening on port 3306) or access cloud metadata endpoints such as http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ on AWS, potentially exposing temporary IAM credentials and allowing further compromise.

Symfony has an implementation for this problem: https://symfony.com/blog/new-in-symfony-5-1-server-side-request-forgery-protection

### Steps To Reproduce

If you have an AWS instance created before around 2024, IMDSv1 is enabled by default. Calling Http::get('http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/...') can return the instance's temporary AWS credentials.

title: `Mail::alwaysTo()` throws an exception for undefined key `name`
author: leandrotijink
body: ### Laravel Version 13.18.1 ### PHP Version 8.5.8 ### Database Driver & Version _No response_ ### Description In a (near) fresh Laravel installation, configuring `alwaysTo` with any value(s) throws an ErrorException with the message `Undefined array key "name"`. It doesn't matter if the second `name` parameter is provided or not. From what I see, the `alwaysTo` method on `Illuminate\Mail\Mailer` is never called, so it must be going wrong before then. Please let me know if additional information is needed. ### Steps To Reproduce Create an empty Laravel project with a basic route returning a string. Define the `Mail::alwaysTo` method with any given address.

title: DB Facades get new DatabaseManager instance when CommandFinished in `config:cache` and `route:cache`
author: Fallsleep

title: Missing cache-headers when using HEAD method
author: c0defre4k
body:
### Laravel Version

12.20.0

### PHP Version

8.4.22

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

This already discussed [here](https://github.com/laravel/framework/issues/47318), but I think this has not been resolved properly and is still an issue.

Imagine you have an endpoint that could take some time to process. You've added cache-headers to allow it being cached by a CDN.
Now doing a HEAD request to that endpoint per default still runs all the code in the controller. The response's content is later [deleted when preparing it](https://github.com/symfony/http-foundation/blob/8.2/Response.php#L283-L289) for the client. Since the response of the HEAD request is missing the Cache-Control header it circumvents the CDN and directly hits the backend. A bad actor could use that to DDoS the your API (assuming that there is not rate-limiting).

The [original issue](https://github.com/laravel/framework/issues/47318) was closed due to the etag header supposedly not applying to HEAD requests. But according to [RFC 9110](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#name-head) they should imho be included:

>  The HEAD method is identical to GET except that the server MUST NOT send content in the response. ŌĆ”
> 
> The server SHOULD send the same header fields in response to a HEAD request as it would have sent if the request method had been GET. However, a server MAY omit header fields for which a value is determined only while generating the content. ŌĆ”

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Use SetCacheHeaders middleware for your routes like this:
```php
// Get also includes HEAD, which makes you assume they both have cache headers.
Route::get('/cacheble-endpoint', [Controller::class, 'endpoint'])->middleware(['cache.headers:public;max_age=3600']);
```
2. Access your routes using GET HTTP method and then HEAD method.
3. Observe the differences in returned HTTP headers.

title: Http Client hardcodes crypto_method TLSv1_2 causing failure on environments with curl < 7.34.0
author: satosix
body:
### Laravel Version

13.16.1

### PHP Version

8.3.30

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

PendingRequest hardcodes crypto_method => STREAM_CRYPTO_METHOD_TLSv1_2_CLIENT as a default option in its constructor:

```
// Illuminate/Http/Client/PendingRequest.php
$this->options = [
    'connect_timeout' => 10,
    'crypto_method' => STREAM_CRYPTO_METHOD_TLSv1_2_CLIENT,
    'http_errors' => false,
    'timeout' => 30,
];
```

On environments where the PHP curl extension is built against curl < 7.34.0, CURL_SSLVERSION_TLSv1_2 is undefined. Guzzle's supportsTls12() check then fails and throws:

```
InvalidArgumentException: Invalid crypto_method request option: TLS 1.2 not supported by your version of cURL
```

Even though TLS 1.2 actually works on the environment (curl --tlsv1.2 https://example.com succeeds).

Related Guzzle issue: https://github.com/guzzle/guzzle/issues/3689

Possible Solution

Option 1 ŌĆö guard with defined():

```
$this->options = [
    'connect_timeout' => 10,
    'crypto_method' => defined('CURL_SSLVERSION_TLSv1_2')
        ? STREAM_CRYPTO_METHOD_TLSv1_2_CLIENT
        : null,
    'http_errors' => false,
    'timeout' => 30,
];
```

Option 2 ŌĆö remove the default entirely and let users opt-in when needed.

Additional context

Workaround: pass 'crypto_method' => null in withOptions() to bypass the check.

```
Http::withOptions(['crypto_method' => null])->get($url);
```

Laravel 13's server requirements do not mention any cURL version requirement,
yet the Http client silently breaks on environments with curl < 7.34.0.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Deploy a Laravel app on an environment with curl 7.29.0 + NSS/3.53.1 (e.g. Xserver shared hosting)
2. Make any HTTP request via Http::get($url)
3. InvalidArgumentException is thrown

title: Default session cookie name differs between the framework default and the laravel/laravel skeleton
author: clarkewing
body:
### Laravel Version

13.16.1 (also on master)

### PHP Version

8.4.22

### Database Driver & Version

_No response_

### Description

The default session cookie name resolves to two different values depending on which default config is in effect, because the framework's bundled `config/session.php` and the first-party application skeleton disagree.

Framework ŌĆö [`laravel/framework` config/session.php](https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/13.x/config/session.php#L130-L133) (what `config:publish session` writes):

```php
Str::snake((string) env('APP_NAME', 'laravel')).'_session'   // e.g. my_app_session
```

Application skeleton ŌĆö [`laravel/laravel` config/session.php](https://github.com/laravel/laravel/blob/13.x/config/session.php#L130-L133) (what `laravel new` ships):

```php
Str::slug((string) env('APP_NAME', 'laravel')).'-session'    // e.g. my-app-session
```

So a fresh application ships with `my-app-session`, but running `php artisan config:publish session` (or slimming the config and falling back to the framework) silently changes it to `my_app_session`, invalidating existing sessions ŌĆö with no application code change.

This appears to stem from #56172, which set the framework default to `Str::snake()`, while the skeleton kept the hyphenated form (an alignment attempt was reverted in laravel/laravel#6646). The cache and Redis prefixes remain consistent between the two (both hyphenated); only the session cookie diverges.

Is this divergence intentional? If not, the framework default and the skeleton should resolve to the same value.

### Steps To Reproduce

With `APP_NAME="My App"`:

1. `laravel new` ships `config/session.php` from the `laravel/laravel` skeleton ŌåÆ
   `config('session.cookie')` resolves to `"my-app-session"` (Str::slug, hyphenated).
2. Run `php artisan config:publish session` (publishes the framework's copy) ŌåÆ
   `config('session.cookie')` now resolves to `"my_app_session"` (Str::snake, underscore).

The cookie name flips from hyphen to underscore separated with no application code change. The same underscore form applies to any app with no published `config/session.php` (framework fallback). Source comparison:
- https://github.com/laravel/framework/blob/13.x/config/session.php#L130-L133
- https://github.com/laravel/laravel/blob/13.x/config/session.php#L130-L133
